title: Monometallic inlet valve for crail engine with hardened tappet end
abstract: This invention comprises a monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine developed in a steel material that can be hardened. The steel material used may be amenable for induction hardening. In one embodiment, the steel material used is Martensitic EN-52 grade steel. In one embodiment, the inlet valve for a CRAIL engine is induction hardened at tappet end. The the tappet end hardness may be 52 HRC min and through hardening depth is 12-16mm with Barrel hardness 490hv30 min. The inlet valve of this invention is designed to perform under common CRAIL engine operating boundary conditions of peak combustion pressure 180 bar and maximum predefined combustion temperature 780Â°C. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to this invention has the Design of Safety of 3.66.
claims: Claims Claims : 1. A monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine developed in a steel material that can be hardened. 2. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 1 , wherein, the steel material is amenable for induction hardening. 3. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 2, wherein the steel material used for making the monometallic inlet valve consists of Martensitic EN-52 grade steel. 4. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 3, wherein the valve is induction hardened at tappet end. 5. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 2, wherein, the inlet valve is designed to perform under common CRAIL engine operating boundary conditions of peak combustion pressure 180 bar and maximum predefined combustion temperature 780Â°C. 6. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 2, wherein, the tappet end hardness is 52 HRC min and through hardening depth is 12-16mm with Barrel hardness 490hv30 min. 7. The monometallic inlet valve for a CRAIL engine according to claim 2 wherein the Design of Safety is 3.66.
